name: Prefix Sum (in-place)
summary: Sweep left to right, adding each element to the one before it so every slot becomes the running total up to that index — one pass, no extra array.
visualizationIntent: init: nums shown as a row of cells; index 0 is already its own running sum (result), the rest idle; pointer i starts at 1. loop (L3): pointer i sits at the current index; cell i-1 flashes compared (the running total being read) and cell i is current (the slot being updated). update (L4): nums[i] += nums[i-1] writes the new running total into cell i — it flashes/pops with the summed value (visible on the next step), then locks to result. return (L5): every cell is now a running sum (all result); pointer parks at the end.

lineExplanations.3: Walk the index from 1 to the end — each pass converts the current slot into a running total. Index 0 is skipped because it is already the sum of everything up to itself.
lineExplanations.4: Add the running total sitting just to the left (nums[i-1]) into the current slot, so nums[i] now holds the sum of every element from the start up to i.
lineExplanations.5: The array has been rewritten in place — every slot is now a running sum — so hand it back.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def runningSum(self, nums): — defines the method; nums is the input list of integers.
syntaxExplanations.3: for i in range(1, len(nums)): — loops i over 1, 2, …, len(nums)-1. Starting at 1 deliberately skips index 0.
syntaxExplanations.4: nums[i] += nums[i - 1] — shorthand for nums[i] = nums[i] + nums[i - 1]; it updates the current slot in place.
syntaxExplanations.5: return nums — hands back the same list object, now filled with the running sums.
